scope: OpenCode routes for GLM 5.2 and Kimi K2.7 Code across Baseten and Fireworks, plus DeepSeek V4 Pro on Baseten

evidence_status.counting_unit: seven judged production-shaped coding cohorts plus two bounded utility workloads
evidence_status.reason: Baseten has retained outcomes across seven production-shaped coding cohorts plus two bounded utility workloads. Two GLM attempts crossed a measured 202,720-token provider input ceiling after OpenCode advertised an incorrect 1,048,576-token catalog context; those completion failures are benchmark-configuration incidents, not model-quality failures. DeepSeek has seven completed judged coding cohorts, including iOS and Android. A Fireworks credential was not present, so matched cross-provider quality, reliability, and realized latency remain pending.

route_definition: model + provider + serving path + reasoning setting

catalog_snapshot.context_values: Catalog values are retained for provenance. The measured Baseten GLM endpoint rejected inputs above 202,720 tokens despite an advertised 1,048,576-token catalog context, so managed runs use the measured operational ceiling instead.

routes.catalog_context_status: incorrect for the measured Baseten serving route
routes.outcome_evidence: matched coding outcomes range from a 91 tooling blind mean to a 58 IaC mean; two context-limited attempts remain eligible for artifact judging but are censored as route-completion evidence because the benchmark used incorrect catalog metadata
unmeasured in the retained matched provider protocol
unmeasured in the retained matched provider protocol

routes.route: Kimi K2.7 Code / Baseten / Standard / default reasoning
routes.outcome_evidence: mixed coding outcomes plus production-ready session titles and unstable project-copy names under the bounded utility protocol

routes.route: Kimi K2.7 Code / Fireworks / Standard / default reasoning
routes.outcome_evidence: unmeasured in the retained matched provider protocol

routes.route: Kimi K2.7 Code / Fireworks / Fast / default reasoning
routes.outcome_evidence: unmeasured in the retained matched provider protocol

routes.route: DeepSeek V4 Pro / Baseten / Standard / default reasoning
routes.outcome_evidence: judged means range from 88.33 on legacy-client permission work and 85.67 on mixed work to 56 on tooling and 48 on IaC; iOS passed validation at 70.33 and led pure Quality:Value but required repair

vendor_time_evidence.claim: Fireworks Fast targets more than 100 generated tokens per second and retains the same model weights as Standard.
vendor_time_evidence.status: vendor claim; not a measured OpenCode result

vendor_time_evidence.claim: Fireworks reports GLM 5.2 Fast at roughly two to three times Standard throughput, with a moving benchmark peak of 446 tokens per second.
vendor_time_evidence.status: vendor snapshot; not a measured OpenCode result

vendor_time_evidence.claim: Baseten reports more than 280 generated tokens per second for GLM 5.2 in its serving benchmark.
vendor_time_evidence.status: vendor snapshot; observed end-to-end wall time must be measured independently

configuration_decision.model_picker: DeepSeek V4 Pro is selectable through Baseten.
configuration_decision.rationale: Historical open-weight outcomes are workload-specific and do not justify a general coding or reader assignment. Existing production defaults remain until repeated iOS-shaped trials establish completion, correctness, cost per valid result, and realized model-session time.

role_assessment.required_open_weight_evidence: separate open-weight promotion gate: three iOS implementation workloads with repeated hidden-test correctness and policy compliance

role_assessment.decision: DeepSeek remains a manual route selection
role_assessment.required_open_weight_evidence: repeat strong route-specific outcomes; historical open-weight results did not transfer uniformly across cohorts
repeated production-shaped planning with blind grading and fatal-safety caps

role_assessment.decision: Kimi remains an explicit bounded reader
role_assessment.required_open_weight_evidence: answer-key citation precision, recall, completion, cost, and latency

role_assessment.decision: use Kimi K2.7 Code through Baseten for the tested output contract
role_assessment.required_open_weight_evidence: re-evaluate when provider, prompt, or output contract changes

role_assessment.decision: retain Luna low; Kimi and GLM require repair or sanitization
role_assessment.required_open_weight_evidence: zero material defects under repeated blind grading

matched_protocol.metadata_gate: Pin OpenCode version and exact provider model IDs; compare effective API IDs, measured provider input ceilings, catalog context claims, capabilities, reasoning variants, interleaving, and system-prompt dispatch. Baseten GLM uses a 202,720-token operational input limit, causing automatic compaction around 182,720 tokens with the retained 20,000-token reserve.
matched_protocol.implementation_screen: Three production-shaped iOS Swift fixtures, one balanced screening run per open-weight route plus contemporaneous Terra; advance only completed, policy-compliant routes.
matched_protocol.implementation_confirmation: Three balanced repeats per advanced route with hidden tests and a locked quality floor.
matched_protocol.planning: Two production-shaped iOS planning clusters, two repeats, contemporaneous Terra, and three blind graders per eligible answer.
matched_protocol.reader: Bounded exact-file questions with answer keys and citation scoring.
decision_order: normalized total cost per valid result
model-session time and time to first observed action
matched_protocol.timing_semantics: OpenCode event timestamps yield launcher startup, time to first observed action, time to first text block, model-session duration, and per-step decision latency. They are not vendor TTFT.
